tool_name: Cooling Tower Performance — Thermal Range, Approach, NTU & Makeup Water Rate

inputs.name: Ambient Wet-Bulb Temperature (T_wb)
inputs.note: Design wet-bulb from ASHRAE 0.4% cooling design data for location

inputs.name: Cycles of Concentration (COC)
inputs.note: COC = TDS_blowdown / TDS_makeup; typical 3–6; higher COC = less water use but more scaling risk

inputs.name: Drift Loss Rate (% of circulating water flow)
inputs.note: Modern fills with drift eliminators: 0.001–0.003%; CTI STD-201 design default

formulas: range_k = T_hot_in_c - T_cold_out_c; approach_k = T_cold_out_c - T_wb_c   // [K] Cooling Range & Approach Temperature Reference: CTI STD-201 RS §3.1 — Range = T_in − T_out; Approach = T_out − T_wb
m_dot_cw_kg_s = Q_rejected_kw / (4.18 * range_k)   // [kg/s] Circulating Water Flow Rate Reference: Energy balance Q = ṁ·c_p·ΔT; c_p = 4.18 kJ/(kg·K)

h_sat_out = 4186 * T_cold_out_c * 0.62198 / (0.622 + 0.62198) + 2501000 * 0.008; h_sat_in = 4186 * T_hot_in_c * 0.62198 / (0.622 + 0.62198) + 2501000 * 0.014; h_air_in = 1006 * T_wb_c + (0.622 + 0.001) * (2501000 + 1860 * T_wb_c); NTU = range_k * 4186 / ((h_sat_in + h_sat_out)/2 - h_air_in)   // [dimensionless] Number of Transfer Units (NTU) — Merkel Equation Approximation Reference: Merkel method (simplified) — CTI STD-201; NTU = c_pw·ΔT / ΔLMHD; typical tower NTU 0.8–2.5
E_loss_kg_s = 0.00085 * range_k * m_dot_cw_kg_s   // [kg/s] Evaporation Loss Rate Reference: CTI §4.2 — evaporation ≈ 0.085% per °C range; rule of thumb for preliminary sizing

drift_kg_s = drift_loss_pct / 100 * m_dot_cw_kg_s; blowdown_kg_s = E_loss_kg_s / (cycles_of_concentration - 1); MU_kg_s = E_loss_kg_s + blowdown_kg_s + drift_kg_s   // [kg/s] Total Makeup Water Requirement Reference: CTI / ASHRAE — makeup = evaporation + blowdown + drift; MU = E/(1 − 1/COC) + D

RT_capacity = Q_rejected_kw / 3.517; kW_per_RT = fan_power_kw / RT_capacity; annual_water_m3 = MU_kg_s * 3600 * 8760 / 1000   // [kW/RT, m³/yr] Tower Energy Performance Ratio (kW/RT) & Annual Water Consumption Reference: ASHRAE 90.1-2022 §6.5.2 — fan power limit; 1 RT = 3.517 kW cooling

V1.error_msg: Approach < 3 K: near theoretical minimum — tower will be very large and sensitive to wet-bulb fluctuation; verify CTI certification data

V2.error_msg: Cold water outlet ≤ wet-bulb temperature: thermodynamically impossible for evaporative cooling — increase T_CW,out above T_wb

V3.error_msg: COC > 7: elevated scaling and corrosion risk — verify water treatment programme and Langelier Saturation Index per ASHRAE

smart_warnings.message: Approach < 5 K: performance highly sensitive to wet-bulb exceedances — model with 99.6% wet-bulb value, not just design point
Fan power > 0.075 kW/RT: above ASHRAE 90.1-2022 fan power limit for centrifugal towers — evaluate VFD on fans for part-load savings
Annual makeup water > 100,000 m³: significant water cost — evaluate side-stream filtration, higher COC, or hybrid dry/wet tower
COC < 3: excessive blowdown — water and chemical waste; increase COC to 4–5 with proper chemical treatment to reduce makeup by 20–30%
Thermal range < 4 K: low-range system requires high water flow — verify pump sizing; consider larger ΔT to reduce circulating flow and pump energy

fmea.effect: Approach < 3 K: near theoretical minimum — tower will be very large and sensitive to wet-bulb fluctuation; verify CTI certification data
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Cold water outlet ≤ wet-bulb temperature: thermodynamically impossible for evaporative cooling — increase T_CW,out above T_wb
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: COC > 7: elevated scaling and corrosion risk — verify water treatment programme and Langelier Saturation Index per ASHRAE
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Approach < 5 K: performance highly sensitive to wet-bulb exceedances — model with 99.6% wet-bulb value, not just design point
Fan power > 0.075 kW/RT: above ASHRAE 90.1-2022 fan power limit for centrifugal towers — evaluate VFD on fans for part-load savings
Annual makeup water > 100,000 m³: significant water cost — evaluate side-stream filtration, higher COC, or hybrid dry/wet tower
COC < 3: excessive blowdown — water and chemical waste; increase COC to 4–5 with proper chemical treatment to reduce makeup by 20–30%
Thermal range < 4 K: low-range system requires high water flow — verify pump sizing; consider larger ΔT to reduce circulating flow and pump energy
